essence: Designs, improves, and oversees CNC‑based manufacturing systems to maximise efficiency, quality, and throughput.

identity.title: CNC Manufacturing Engineer
identity.summary: Develops manufacturing processes, integrates CNC technologies, improves workflow, and ensures production meets engineering, quality, and cost targets across the full manufacturing lifecycle.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, defence, robotics, or high‑precision manufacturing can exceed £95k-£130k+.

qualifications: Degree or experience in manufacturing engineering, mechanical engineering, or CNC process engineering
Strong understanding of CNC machining, tooling, materials, and tolerances
Knowledge of lean manufacturing, workflow optimisation, and quality systems
Ability to design processes, improve efficiency, and integrate new technologies

lifestyle.description: CNC manufacturing engineers work in aerospace, automotive, robotics, and advanced manufacturing. They design and optimise machining processes, integrate automation, reduce waste, and ensure production meets engineering and business goals. The role suits individuals who enjoy systems thinking, optimisation, and engineering leadership.

habits: Designs and improves CNC manufacturing processes
Optimises workflow, tooling, and cycle times
Implements lean manufacturing and continuous improvement
Collaborates with machinists, programmers, and quality teams
Integrates automation, robotics, and new CNC technologies
